children.text: Davenport Point is a compact waterfront neighborhood in Stamford that feels tucked away. The peninsula setting creates a strong sense of separation from busier parts of the city, and day-to-day life here tends to be quieter and more residential.

Real estate in Davenport Point is primarily single-family, and the character leans coastal: mature landscaping, a water-oriented layout, and a neighborhood rhythm that’s more “weekend by the Sound” than “in the middle of it.” Depending on the property, buyers may find water views, outdoor entertaining space, and a sense of privacy that’s hard to replicate in more central neighborhoods.

For outdoor time close to home, John J. Boccuzzi Park is nearby at 200 Southfield Avenue—a 12-acre coastal park with a beach and recreation facilities. And when you want restaurants, events, and Metro-North access, downtown Stamford remains a straightforward drive away. Davenport Point is best suited for clients who want a distinctly coastal setting without giving up Stamford convenience.

highlights: A quiet peninsula-style waterfront setting with a removed, peaceful atmosphere
Primarily single-family coastal properties with Long Island Sound views
Nearby parks with beaches, marina, and recreation facilities
Close to Cove Island Park for walking, beaches, and outdoor time
Easy drive to downtown Stamford dining, arts, and Metro-North service
name: Davenport Point
overview: Davenport Point is a small, peninsula-style waterfront enclave in Stamford, defined by a quiet residential setting and a strong connection to Long Island Sound. Homes here are predominantly single-family, with architecture ranging from classic coastal styles to newer, updated properties—some with direct water views. The neighborhood appeals to buyers who prioritize privacy and a maritime feel, but still want practical access to downtown Stamford and nearby amenities. It’s one of those Stamford pockets where the setting does much of the talking.
